title: Senior Library and Knowledge Services Assistant
how_to_become: To become a senior library and knowledge services assistant, you'll need relevant experience working in a health library environment, or 4 years of experience working in a library environment.

salary.entry_note: Senior library and knowledge services assistants in the NHS start at £25,694 before tax.
salary.experienced_note: Senior library and knowledge services assistants in the NHS can earn up to £41,424 before tax.

description: Senior library and knowledge services assistants provide support to the wider knowledge team. They take part in a range of daily tasks to keep systems timely and accurate, and provide specialist information and knowledge to service users accessing the library's services.
role_overview: As a senior library and knowledge services assistant, you'll be organising and maintaining library resources and providing specialist knowledge services to users.

entry_requirements.overview: 5 National 5 qualifications including English and Maths are required, or an HNC or HND level qualification. Alternatively, 4 years of experience working in a library environment is accepted.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to college or a new job with NHSScotland. You'll get training, increase your knowledge, and learn new skills.

education_and_training.alternative_entry: 4 years of experience working in a library environment is also accepted as an entry route.

learning_and_development.overview: You will be encouraged to develop your career in the NHS. As well as learning specific to your role, you'll need to complete mandatory training.
learning_and_development.note: Your NHS employer will confirm their mandatory training requirements during your induction.

career_progression.overview: With experience you could progress to more senior library and knowledge roles.

professional_bodies: Chartered Institute of Library and Information Professionals (CILIP)

chunks.text: To become a senior library and knowledge services assistant, you'll need relevant experience in a health library environment, or 4 years of experience working in a library environment.
Senior library and knowledge services assistants in the NHS start at £25,694 before tax and can earn up to £41,424 before tax at experienced level.

Senior library and knowledge services assistants support the wider knowledge team, carrying out daily tasks to keep systems accurate and providing specialist information and knowledge to library service users.
As a senior library and knowledge services assistant, you'll organise and maintain library resources and provide specialist knowledge services to users.

Entry requires 5 National 5 qualifications including English and Maths, or an HNC or HND level qualification. Alternatively, 4 years of library experience is accepted. Useful school subjects include English, Maths, Information Systems, and Business Studies.
Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to college or a new job with NHSScotland.
College courses include Library and Information Services HNC and Administration and Information Technology HNC. Alternatively, 4 years of library experience is accepted as an entry route.
Mandatory training includes occupational health, health and safety, fire safety, counter-fraud, moving and handling, and safe information handling. Requirements are confirmed during induction.

With experience, senior library and knowledge services assistants can progress to clinical librarian. Staff can also work towards CILIP certification, which provides access to further training and development.

Senior library and knowledge services assistants may work towards the Chartered Institute of Library and Information Professionals (CILIP) certification, which provides access to further training and development as a knowledge services professional.
